perturbation.original_text: 3. Disclosure . All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure , except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity; (b) be disclosed only to employees of P a r t y T w o who have executed written confidentiality agreements wit h the employer; and (c) be reproduced or used by P a r t y T w o only to the extent necessary to fulfill it’s obligations hereunder. Party One and P a r t y Two shall protect shared Confidential Information with the same degree of care as it normally exercises to protect its own confidential information of similar nature, but at a minimum with a reasonable degree of care to prevent its unauthorized use, dissemination or publication. Each party shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information of the other par ty where compelled by law to so disclose pursuant to any legal, judicial or administrative proceeding, provided the disclosing party provides all reasonable prior notice to the other party to allow it to seek a protective order or other appropriate relief. Party One and P a r t y T w o pledges and agrees that it shall keep totally confidential any and all of the said information and other such matters arising hereto, and shall not disclose to any other person or persons, corporation, agent or consultant with whom it is or may be associated with, any such confidential information without express permission being given in writing by an authorized officer of other Party .
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Disclosure . All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure ; (b) be disclosed only to employees of P a r t y T w o who have executed written confidentiality agreements wit h the employer; and (c) be reproduced or used by P a r t y T w o only to the extent necessary to fulfill it’s obligations hereunder. Party One and P a r t y Two shall protect shared Confidential Information with the same degree of care as it normally exercises to protect its own confidential information of similar nature, but at a minimum with a reasonable degree of care to prevent its unauthorized use, dissemination or publication. Each party shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information of the other par ty where compelled by law to so disclose pursuant to any legal, judicial or administrative proceeding, provided the disclosing party provides all reasonable prior notice to the other party to allow it to seek a protective order or other appropriate relief. Party One and P a r t y T w o pledges and agrees that it shall keep totally confidential any and all of the said information and other such matters arising hereto, and shall not disclose to any other person or persons, corporation, agent or consultant with whom it is or may be associated with, any such confidential information without express permission being given in writing by an authorized officer of other Party .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity', Section 3 creates a contradiction with the later enforcement of protecting proprietary interests in Section 10. Section 10 explicitly references the agreement's provisions as necessary to protect 'proprietary interests', but the removal of the clause ensuring perpetual protection for trade secrets and research contradicts this commitment. This weakens the enforceability of protecting those specific types of information, as the three-year limit now applies to everything.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies . Party One and P a r t y T w o each acknowledge that compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is necessary to protec t their proprietary interes ts. Each party further acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure to any person or entity in breach of this Agreement will result in irreparable and continuing damage, and that each party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain immediate injun ctive relief and any other rights or remedies to which it may be entitled. If either party violates any of the above agreements, it acknowledges that money damages will be an inadequate remedy and that the violated party will be entitled to specific perfo rmance or to injunctive relief to prohibit the violating party from continuing to violate this agreement even if no money damages can be proven.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause ensuring perpetual confidentiality for trade secrets and Party One's R&D information. This directly contradicts Section 10, which states that compliance with the agreement is necessary to protect proprietary interests. Since the three-year confidentiality limit now applies to all information, it weakens the enforceability of protecting Party One's trade secrets and R&D, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Property Rights, Return of Materials . All materials, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Property Rights, Return of Materials . All materials, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement , return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'or request by the other party' from Section 6, which concerns the return of materials, creates an in-text contradiction with the expectations set in Section 4 regarding the use of confidential information. Section 4 explicitly states the need for 'express written permission' for disclosure. However, omitting the 'or request by the other party' clause in Section 6 implies materials do not need to be immediately returned upon request which creates ambiguity if upon request for return of materials is it a violation of Section 4. This weakens the party's control over their confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Use. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to use the other party's Confidential Information for its own use or for any purpose except for the purpose described in Section 1. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others, without the express written permission of the other party .
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'or request by the other party' in Section 6, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation, directly contradicts Section 4. Section 4 requires 'express written permission' for disclosing confidential information. The change in Section 6, omitting the return of materials upon request, creates ambiguity and weakens the party's control over confidential information, creating a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language was removed creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Information Not Confidential. Information shall be deemed not to be confidential if such information is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party, or is already known by the receiving party as evidenced by competent proof thereof, or is approved for rel ease by the prior written approval of the disclosing party, or is rightfully received by the receiving party from a third party without restriction and without breach of this agreement, or is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a sim ilar restriction on the rights of such third party, or is independently developed by the receiving party without the use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Information Not Confidential. Information shall be deemed not to be confidential if such information is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party, or is already known by the receiving party as evidenced by competent proof thereof, or is approved for rel ease by the prior written approval of the disclosing party, or is rightfully received by the receiving party from a third party without restriction and without breach of this agreement, or is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a sim ilar restriction on the rights of such third party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'or is independently developed by the receiving party without the use of the Confidential Information' from Section 8, it creates an inconsistency with Section 5 concerning 'Future Development.' Without this exclusion, any independently developed item after exposure to confidential information could be wrongly interpreted as a violation of Section 5, regardless of whether the confidential information was actually used. This could lead to disputes over independently created products, making Section 5's enforcement uncertain in such cases.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Future Development. P a r t y T w o shall not directly or indirectly acquire any interest in, or design, create, manufacture, sell or otherwise deal with any item or product, based upon or derived from the Confidential Information provided by Party One , except as may be expressly agreed to in writing by Party One .
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 8 excludes independently developed information from being considered confidential. The changed text removes this exclusion, creating a direct contradiction with Section 5. Section 5 prohibits Party Two from dealing with any item or product derived from the Confidential Information of Party One. Without the exclusion present in the original Section 8, independently developed products could be wrongly interpreted as a violation of Section 5, even if the Confidential Information was not used. This inconsistency creates a clear conflict, impacting the enforceability and interpretation of both sections.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Non-solicitation: Each party agrees that, for a period of 18 months following the date of this Agree ment, it shall not solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with any of the officers, employees, directors or consultants of the other party which whom such party has had contact or were identified to such party in connection with its evalu ation of a possible transaction between the parties. The term “solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with” shall not be deemed to include generalized searches by Party One for employees or consultants through media advertisements, emplo yment firms or otherwise, that are not focused on persons employed by or who consult for P a r t y T w o .
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Non-solicitation: Each party agrees that, for a period of 18 months following the date of this Agree ment, it shall not solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with any of the officers, employees, directors or consultants of the other party. The term “solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with” shall not be deemed to include generalized searches by Party One for employees or consultants through media advertisements, emplo yment firms or otherwise, that are not focused on persons employed by or who consult for P a r t y T w o .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'which whom such party has had contact or were identified to such party in connection with its evalu ation of a possible transaction between the parties' in Section 9, it creates a contradiction with Section 1, the purpose clause. Section 1's purpose is about protecting confidential information during a specific business relationship evaluation. Removing the clause about contact during this evaluation broadens the non-solicitation restriction beyond the scope defined in the purpose clause. This means you could not solicit employees you never had contact with during the evaluation. This may contradict the initial understanding and intended limitations of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose . The purpose of this Agreement is to protect the Confidential Information of the parties, as defined below, which is being disclosed for the purpose of a business relationship between the parties. Party One and P a r t y T w o include its family members, associates, employees, agents, or any other such person or persons individually as well as collectively.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 9 limited the non-solicitation to individuals the party had contact with or were identified during the evaluation. The changed text removes this limitation, broadening the scope of non-solicitation to potentially include any officer, employee, director, or consultant of the other party, regardless of whether there was contact during the evaluation. This contradicts Section 1, the purpose clause, which states the agreement's purpose is to protect confidential information disclosed for 'a business relationship between the parties.' By broadening the non-solicitation clause, it extends beyond the scope of protecting information within that defined business relationship, creating a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Term . This agreement shall r emain in effect for a period of Three (3) Years or the life of any Agreements, Contracts or Commercial Arrangements which may arise from the joint efforts of the parties hereto, whichever shall be longer.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Term . This agreement shall r emain in effect for a period of Three (3) Years.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'or the life of any Agreements, Contracts or Commercial Arrangements which may arise from the joint efforts of the parties hereto, whichever shall be longer' from Section 15 creates an contradiction. Section 5 restricts 'Future Development' based on confidential information. However, by setting a hard 3-year limit to the whole document it would contradict with Section 5. Because it says that that Party Two cannot directly or indirectly acquire any interest in, or design, create, manufacture, sell or otherwise deal with any item or product, based upon or derived from the Confidential Information provided by Party One. After the 3 years period would party two be able to do what is forbidden during the 3 year limit?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Future Development. P a r t y T w o shall not directly or indirectly acquire any interest in, or design, create, manufacture, sell or otherwise deal with any item or product, based upon or derived from the Confidential Information provided by Party One , except as may be expressly agreed to in writing by Party One .
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'or the life of any Agreements, Contracts or Commercial Arrangements which may arise from the joint efforts of the parties hereto, whichever shall be longer' from Section 15 creates a strong contradiction. The original text allowed for the agreement to extend beyond three years based on ongoing commercial arrangements. The changed text sets a hard 3-year limit. This contradicts Section 5, which restricts Party Two's ability to use confidential information indefinitely unless expressly agreed to in writing by Party One. With the 3-year limit, the restriction in Section 5 becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable after that period, creating a clear conflict regarding Party Two's obligations after the 3-year term. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
